changes_made: Renamed k8s_pod_backup_annotation_present to k8s_pod_backup_strategy_documented for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the PCI DSS control 3.1.1, which focuses on data protection and backup strategies. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but one function required renaming for better clarity and alignment with the intended check. The functions are technically accurate and applicable to Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: k8s_pod_backup_annotation_present should be renamed to k8s_pod_backup_strategy_documented to better reflect the purpose of ensuring backup strategies are documented.
title: Requirement 3.1.1
step1_reasoning: The PCI DSS control 3.1.1 is focused on ensuring that data is protected through lifecycle management and backup strategies. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring etcd (which stores cluster state and secrets) is properly backed up and encrypted. Additionally, ensuring that secrets are encrypted at rest aligns with data protection requirements. While Kubernetes does not inherently manage application data backups, annotations or labels can be used to ensure that pods have backup strategies documented. Audit log retention ensures that changes can be tracked and are available for compliance verification.
